name: West Side Trnsprtn Bus Svc
districts_or_customers: Possible small contractor in Mankato Area Public Schools (ISD 77) territory; ISD 77 contracts primarily with Palmer Bus Service (north of old Hwy 14) and Yaeger Bus Service (south of old Hwy 14) — West Side Transportation Bus Service does not appear on the district's named contractor list.
recent_news: No M&A or operational news found. Listing appears to be a very small/sole-proprietor record on Hwy 14 west of Mankato; not corroborated by district or news sources.
fit_notes: Too small and unverified to be a meaningful Summit tuck-in (2 employees, ~$83K revenue). Mankato market is already served by larger contractors (Palmer, Yaeger). Pass unless additional fleet/contract evidence surfaces.

name: Dyson Bus Svc Inc
principals: Founded by Roland Dyson (deceased Nov 2021); part of Dyson family group (Celestine C. Dyson Inc., Dyson Bus Services Inc., Dyson Trucking Inc. — treated as single employer in NLRB case 05-RC-288786)
districts_or_customers: Charles County Public Schools (CCPS) — long-tenured contractor; member of Charles County School Bus Contractors Association
recent_news: NLRB representation case 05-RC-288786 (2022) treated Celestine C. Dyson Inc., Dyson Bus Services Inc., and Dyson Trucking Inc. as a single employer of CCPS drivers. Founder Roland A. Dyson died Nov 2021; succession transition underway under family members. Operates within the new multi-year CCPS contractor framework negotiated 2023-2024.
fit_notes: Genuine family-owned legacy CCPS contractor (since 1971), founder recently deceased — classic succession signal. Part of a 3-entity Dyson family group (bus + trucking) that could be acquired together. Worth a direct outreach to family principals; CCPS is a stable multi-year contracted district. Strong fit for Summit's MD/DC corridor tuck-in thesis.

name: Hafermann Bus Co
owner_or_parent: independent (Hafermann family); operated as owner-operator within Prior Lake School Bus Association
districts_or_customers: Prior Lake-Savage Area Schools (ISD 719) via the Prior Lake School Bus Association (PLSBA), the cooperative through which independent owner-operators have historically served the district
recent_news: Owner Jerry Hafermann passed away January 10, 2024 (age 88). He was an owner-operator with the Prior Lake Bus Association. Status of the Hafermann Bus Co. operation post-death is unclear from public sources; likely wound down or transferred within PLSBA.
fit_notes: Likely too small / now defunct or absorbed within PLSBA cooperative after Jerry Hafermann's 2024 death. Not a standalone acquirable entity. The more relevant Prior Lake target would be Busse Student Transportation or PLSBA itself, not Hafermann.

name: Held Bus Svc
owner_or_parent: Faribault Transportation Service (part of the Minnesota Coaches group, now controlled by Tiger Infrastructure Partners Fund IV via TIP Minnesota Coaches Acquisition LLC, Jan 2025)
principals: Former owner Jon Held (sold 2025); prior generation Howard Held
districts_or_customers: Kenyon-Wanamingo Public Schools (K-W ISD 2172) — contract transitioned to Faribault Transportation Services upon sale
recent_news: SOLD in 2025 to Faribault Transportation Company; K-W school board approved 10-year contract with Faribault Transportation in lieu of Held Bus. Faribault Transportation itself is part of Patrick O. Regan's MN bus group (acquired control Nov 2024), which was then acquired by Tiger Infrastructure Partners Fund IV (Federal Register notice Jan 8, 2025).
fit_notes: NOT a target — already acquired (2025) by Faribault Transportation, which is itself under Tiger Infrastructure Partners (a competing PE-backed student-transport platform). Remove from pipeline. The transaction itself is informative: Tiger is actively rolling up MN family bus operators, signaling competitive pressure for Summit in the Upper Midwest.

name: L & R Transportation
districts_or_customers: Sunnyside, WA area (Yakima Valley); not listed on Sunnyside School District named contractor pages — Sunnyside SD operates its own transportation department, so L&R likely serves private/charter/SPED runs or neighboring small districts rather than the main district
recent_news: No M&A or operational news found. SAFER/FMCSA registration exists for L&R Transportation LLC (USDOT 3490676). Yelp/YellowPages listings exist; otherwise minimal public footprint.
fit_notes: Very small (2 employees, ~$75K revenue) with no confirmed district contract; Sunnyside SD self-operates transport. Likely a micro charter/SPED operator. Not a meaningful tuck-in for Summit at this scale; deprioritize unless Yakima Valley roll-up strategy emerges.
